claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin determining your eligibility for Child Benefit.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

Could you please tell me:
1. **The name(s) of the child/children** you want to claim Child Benefit for
2. **The age(s)** of each child

This will help me determine which eligibility rules apply to your situation. 

- If the child is **under 16**, they may automatically qualify
- If the child is **16 to 19**, we'll need to check their education or training status
- If the child is **20 or over**, they generally won't qualify for Child Benefit

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Oh, hello! Yes, I'm wanting to find out about claiming Child Benefit for my three children.

I've got Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 10.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely to meet you! Thank you for providing those details. You have three children:

- **Alex** - age 3
- **Blake** - age 8
- **Charlie** - age 10

Great news! Since all three of your children are **under 16**, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Now I need to check a few more things to determine your full eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Now I need to check a few conditions. Since your children are all very young (ages 3, 8, and 10), I can safely assume they are **not working 24 or more hours per week** in paid employment. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right – none of them work at all. They're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Next question:

**Are your children receiving any benefits in their own name?**

Specifically, I'm asking whether any of them receive Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own right. Given their young ages (3, 8, and 10), I'd expect the answer is no, but I just need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them get any benefits in their own names. They're too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's right! Next question:

**Are any of your children currently doing an apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (3, 8, and 10), I expect they're not, but I just need to confirm that none of them are in any kind of apprenticeship programme.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not – they're far too young for apprenticeships!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course! Now let's check your residency status.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK – I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, Manchester! That confirms you normally live in the UK. ✓

Now I need to check your responsibility for the children.

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies:

1. **You live with the children** – this is the most common situation
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** – paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** – under a court order or agreement that covers their upkeep

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex and Charlie live with me at home. But Blake doesn't live with me – Blake lives with their dad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So to summarise:

- **Alex (age 3)** – lives with you ✓
- **Charlie (age 10)** – lives with you ✓
- **Blake (age 8)** – lives with their dad

For **Alex and Charlie**, since they live with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement.

For **Blake**, since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you contribute to Blake's upkeep?**

This could include:
- Paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards Blake's care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
- Making maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Or are you only looking to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie (the two who live with you)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not really contributing anything financially for Blake at the moment, no.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being upfront about that.

Based on what you've told me, for **Blake** you wouldn't meet the responsibility requirement since Blake doesn't live with you and you're not currently contributing to Blake's upkeep. This means you wouldn't be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

However, **Blake's dad** (who Blake lives with) may be able to claim Child Benefit for Blake instead.

Let's continue the assessment for **Alex and Charlie**, who both live with you. ✓

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about other claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

If someone else is claiming, do you know if they would agree to transfer the claim to you, or could there be a dispute about who should receive it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming for Alex or Charlie. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's straightforward then. ✓

Now I need to check about where Alex and Charlie are currently living.

**Are Alex and Charlie living at home with you under normal circumstances?**

Or is either of them currently:
- In **hospital** for an extended stay
- In **local authority care**
- In a **foster care** arrangement

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie are both living at home with me, yes. But actually, Blake has been in hospital for a while now – a few months, I think.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I hope Blake is doing okay and recovers well.

That's helpful information about Blake. However, since Blake lives with their dad (not with you) and you're not contributing financially to Blake's care, the hospital situation wouldn't affect your claim – it would be relevant for Blake's dad if he were claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

For **Alex and Charlie**, since they're both living at home with you under normal circumstances, that's perfect. ✓

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! One final question:

**Is the local council paying anything towards Alex or Charlie's accommodation or maintenance?**

For example, is the council contributing to their housing costs or care? (This would be unusual if they're simply living at home with you, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for them. They just live with me as normal.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Great news! Here's your Child Benefit eligibility result:

### ✅ ELIGIBLE for Alex and Charlie

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 3 | ✅ Yes | Lives with you, meets all criteria |
| **Charlie** | 10 | ✅ Yes | Lives with you, meets all criteria |
| **Blake** | 8 | ❌ No (for you) | Lives with dad, no financial contribution from you |

---

### Next Steps to Claim for Alex and Charlie:

1. **Apply online** at [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim) or by post using **form CH2**
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and the children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Charlie
4. Payments are typically made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### ⚠️ Important Note:
If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through tax.

---

### About Blake:
While you can't claim Child Benefit for Blake yourself, **Blake's dad** (who Blake lives with) may well be eligible to claim. You might want to let him know so Blake doesn't miss out.

I hope Blake recovers well soon. 💙

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) is ELIGIBLE: Alex is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own name, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), no other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Alex is living at home under normal circumstances, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 8) is NOT ELIGIBLE for this claimant: Although Blake is under 16 and would otherwise meet the child-related criteria, the claimant does not meet the 'responsibility for child' requirement. Blake does not live with the claimant (Blake lives with their dad) and the claimant is not contributing financially to Blake's upkeep. Therefore, this claimant cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. However, Blake's dad may be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie (age 10) is ELIGIBLE: Charlie is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own name, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), no other person is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie is living at home under normal circumstances, and the local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for two of their three children. Alex (age 3) and Charlie (age 10) both live with the claimant, meet all eligibility criteria, and the claimant can claim Child Benefit for them. Blake (age 8) lives with their other parent (dad), and the claimant does not contribute financially to Blake's care, so the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. Blake's dad should be advised to check his own eligibility to claim for Blake.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)
